stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump urges Tehran to ‘give up’; Ghalibaf mocks US strategy
stories.summary: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf ridicules US's economic pressure campaign as oil prices surge and Hormuz standoff continues.

stories.headline: Trump says he is weighing reducing US troop presence in Germany after Iran feud with Merz
stories.summary: Trump's threat comes after Merz earlier this week said that Washington was being “humiliated” by the Iranian leadership, criticising the US lack of strategy in the war.

stories.headline: Hegseth quizzed for nearly six hours on Iran in first congressional appearance since war began
stories.summary: Hegseth quizzed for nearly six hours on Iran war in first congressional appearance since war began

stories.headline: Oil jumps to highest price since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options
stories.summary: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

stories.headline: Kyiv Mayor on Challenges Posed by Russia's Invasion
stories.summary: Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said the Ukrainian capital is in a better shape to face next winter after Russian strikes caused mass power outages during this season’s prolonged sub-zero temperatures. Klitschko spoke to Bloomberg from the 12th Bloomberg CityLab Summit in Madrid. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bondi royal commission: report calls for better policing of Jewish festivals after ‘high’ terror risk flagged for Hanukah event
stories.summary: Interim report contains 14 recommendations, after inquiry examined agencies’ actions before the attack in which 15 people were killedFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastSydney’s Jewish community told police of a “high” threat of an attack at December’s Chanukah by the Sea festival at Bondi beach, but New South Wales police appeared not to have completed a comprehensive r

stories.headline: Bondi shooting inquiry calls for gun reform and more security at Jewish festivals
stories.summary: An interim report says country should prioritise 'nationally consistent' firearms reforms.

stories.headline: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel
stories.summary: The governor of Sinaloa, Rúben Rocha Moya, hails from the same party as the president of Mexico.

stories.headline: Demand at Japan’s Two-Year Note Sale Jumps to Highest Since 2024
stories.summary: Japan’s two-year government bond auction on Thursday drew the strongest demand since August 2024, supported by higher yields and expectations that the Bank of Japan may not rush into further interest rate hikes.

stories.headline: ECB Set to Hold Interest Rates as It Bides Time on Iran Response
stories.summary: The European Central Bank is set to keep interest rates unchanged on Thursday as it continues to calibrate its response to the Iran war’s economic fallout.

stories.headline: Lenders HSBC, NAB Earnings Outlook Dampened by Middle East War Risks
stories.summary: Asia Pacific’s main lenders will offer diverging paths navigating the fallout of the Iran war, with HSBC Holdings Plc and National Australia Bank Ltd. more exposed than Singaporean peers.

stories.headline: Australia news live: NT police find body of child in search for missing five-year-old girl
stories.summary: NT police commissioner says investigators believe the body to be that of missing girl. stories.headline: 'Everything had been looted': Myanmar's civil war takes heavy toll
Analysis: How the Shenzhen APEC summit might reshape Japan-China ties
Google outpaces rivals as Big Tech’s AI spending plans rise to $725bn
stories.summary: Meta stock drops on capex increase while Alphabet’s cloud business grows faster than rivals Amazon and Microsoft

stories.headline: India’s Rupee Drops to Record Low as Crude Surge Deepens Worries
stories.summary: India’s rupee slid to a record low as crude prices continued their relentless surge, deepening worries about the nation’s external deficit.

stories.headline: Leo Wealth's Mironenko on ETF's Role in Portfolio
stories.summary: Aleksey Mironenko, Global Head of Investment Solutions at Leo Wealth, discusses his strategy for selecting ETFs for his clients' needs. ETFs make up about 60% of his portfolios. He discussed all this on Bloomberg ETF IQ Asia. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Activity Data Has Been 'Fairly Resilient': Citigroup
stories.summary: Johanna Chua of Citigroup Global Markets says the recent US activity data "has been fairly resilient". She tells Bloomberg Television that having "three dissenters against the easing bias" at the Federal Reserve is not "a huge surprise" but marks an interesting shift.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Record Lows Sweep Asian Currencies as Oil Spike Revives Risks
stories.summary: A renewed rise in oil prices past $120 a barrel is intensifying pressure on some of Asia’s most fragile currencies, with several trading back at all-time lows.

stories.headline: JPX CEO: Stable FX To Attract Global Money
stories.summary:
Hiromi Yamaji, CEO of Japan Exchange Group, says a stable exchange rate is the best way for attracting global money, and discusses his outlook for the exchange's business and growth for the year.
He speaks exclusively with Shery Ahn on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Indian Stocks’ April Rally Masks Fragile Underpinnings
stories.summary: Higher oil prices, persistent foreign outflows pose risks to the market.

stories.headline: House Adopts Budget to Unlock $70 Billion for Immigration Enforcement
stories.summary: The measure will allow the G.O.P. to begin working on a filibuster-proof bill to fund ICE and C.B.P., part of their plan to reopen the long-shuttered Department of Homeland Security.

Meta raises 2026 capex to $145 billion amid AI spending boost
China's technological ambitions hinge on property market

Ukraine asks Israel to seize vessel it claims is carrying grain stolen by Russia

stories.headline: Ukraine asks Israel to seize ship carrying grain ‘stolen’ by Russia
stories.summary: Kyiv requested that Israel seize the vessel and its cargo, take grain ‌samples, and question crew ​members.

stories.headline: Hegseth clashes with lawmakers over Iran war in nearly six-hour-long hearing
stories.summary: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth sparred with Democratic lawmakers during a nearly six-hour-long hearing, in his first time facing questions under oath since the start of the Iran war.

stories.headline: Oil Hits Wartime High on Report US Eyeing Iran Military Options
stories.summary: Brent oil rallied to a wartime high after Axios reported that US President Donald Trump is set to receive a briefing on new military options for action in Iran, signaling the potential for fresh escalation in the Middle East.

stories.headline: U.S. aircraft carrier to leave Mideast, reducing military might amid Iran war
stories.summary: The USS Gerald R. Ford, at sea for 10 months, is in need of repair. Its exit, though, reduces the firepower on hand as Trump presses Tehran to make peace.

stories.headline: US lawmakers question Pete Hegseth over budget for war on Iran
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before Congress on the Pentagon’s budget for the US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.headline: Pentagon chief Hegseth first public hearing on Iran war: Key takeaways
stories.summary: Pete Hegseth testifies alongside General Dan Caine, responds to questions on war Pentagon says has cost $25bn.

stories.headline: Ukraine formally asks Israel to seize vessel carrying grain stolen by Russia
stories.summary: A Ukrainian source told Euronews that "if Israel responded to other requests via diplomatic channels, there would have been no need for tweets," after Israel's foreign minister lashed out over what he called "Twitter diplomacy."

stories.headline: Second US ambassador to Ukraine steps down in less than one year
stories.summary: The acting US ambassador to Ukraine is to step down from her post in Kyiv and retire, the US State Department announced. Reports have indicated her reasons for leaving the job are related to the Trump administration's approach to Ukraine.

stories.headline: Calls for humanitarian corridor through strait of Hormuz as Iran war hits vital aid
stories.summary: Soaring oil prices and the blockade are preventing food, fuel and medicine being delivered to millions of people in desperate need, say NGOsThe volatility of global oil prices caused by the US and Israel’s war on Iran is taking a toll on the most vulnerable people, by slowing or blocking food and medical aid from reaching them.Now aid organisations are calling for a “humanitarian corridor” to be opened through the strait of Hormuz amid rocketing

stories.headline: Trump threatens to reduce troop numbers in Germany amid growing row with Nato allies
stories.summary: US president’s threat comes after Germany’s Friedrich Merz suggests Trump team is being outplayed in its negotiations with IranThe US may reduce its number of troops deployed in Germany, Donald Trump has announced, days after the country’s chancellor said America was being “humiliated” by Iran.In a post on his Truth Social platform, the US president said his administration was “studying and reviewing the possible reduction of troops in Germany, w

stories.headline: EU loosens state aid rules as Middle East crisis hits energy costs
stories.summary: Brussels loosens state aid rules as Middle East turmoil drives up fuel and fertiliser costs, allowing governments to cushion farmers, hauliers and industry with emergency subsidies.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.headline: New images show suspect taking selfies before Washington press dinner shooting
stories.summary: Prosecutors argue Cole Tomas Allen should remain in custody until trial on a charge of attempted assassination of President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.summary: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.headline: How the Voting Rights Act Helped Latinos in California
stories.summary: The federal law had been a “game changer” for Latino voters, but Wednesday’s ruling could weaken Asian Americans’ political power in the state.

stories.headline: Supreme Court limits key provision of the landmark Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The decision could touch off a scramble by Republicans to redraw majority-minority congressional districts, especially in the South, that could cost many Black Democrats their seats.

stories.headline: Anthony Albanese rules out gas export tax on existing contracts and criticises ‘populist’ campaign
stories.summary: Prime minister says the middle of a global fuel crisis is ‘the worst possible time to jeopardise’ Australia’s partnerships with Asian trading partnersFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAnthony Albanese has confirmed next month’s federal budget will not include a new tax on existing gas export contracts as he criticised the “populist” campaign calling for a levy on produ

stories.headline: Why is the UAE quitting OPEC – and what’s the impact?
stories.summary: The decision by the United Arab Emirates comes during the worst global energy crisis of modern times.

stories.headline: Ex-FBI chief Comey appears in court over alleged Trump threat
stories.summary: Comey turned himself in on two charges, threatening the US president's life and transmitting threats across state lines.

stories.headline: US Federal Reserve holds rates as Jerome Powell signals he will stay on
stories.summary: A divided US Federal Reserve has held interest rates steady for a third consecutive meeting, as policymakers grapple with heightened uncertainty linked to the war in the Middle East. The decision is expected to be the last overseen by Chair Jerome Powell.

stories.headline: Italy says it has filed to be civil party in probe into Swiss bar fire that killed 41
stories.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has vowed justice for Italian victims while Italy has opened its own investigation into the fire even as the Swiss probe continues.

stories.headline: London Stock Exchange adds first Ukrainian firms to reconstruction fund
stories.summary: For the first time Ukrainian equities have been included in a London-listed fund, which aims to channel investment into the nation’s eventual post-war reconstruction and current economic resilience.

stories.headline: Madagascar detains French national over alleged plot to stir unrest
stories.summary: Malagasy prosecuters used a Whatsapp group as evidence for the detained individuals alleged crimes.

stories.headline: Irish government announces further fuel supports after protests
stories.summary: The Irish government has announced further fuel supports for a number of sectors after nationwide protests in recent weeks.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: SoftBank plans to list new AI and robotics company in the US
stories.summary: Masayoshi Son plots IPO for business named Roze as soon as this year

stories.headline: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square USA slides on Wall Street debut
stories.summary: Weak investor demand hits hedge fund chief’s scaled-back double offering

stories.headline: Yen Slides Past 160 Per Dollar to Weakest Level Since 2024
stories.summary: The yen extended its slide beyond 160 per dollar to its weakest mark this year, fueling risk that Japanese officials may step into the market to offer support.

stories.headline: King Charles urges checks on executive power as Trump hosts royal visit
stories.summary: In a speech to Congress, the monarch avoided naming the president but highlighted democratic norms, Ukraine and Western unity.

stories.summary: Human rights commissioner says alleged jailing highlights the ‘growing risks of transnational repression’ in AustraliaFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAustralia’s human rights commissioner has said the Chinese student who was allegedly jailed for six years by Chinese authorities for joining protests in Sydney underscores the “very real and growing risks of transnation

stories.headline: More private health records of UK Biobank volunteers appear on Chinese website
stories.summary: Patrick Vallance says government working with Chinese officials to remove postings from Alibaba after Biobank data breach last weekThere have been further listings of confidential health records of UK volunteers on the Chinese website Alibaba since the breach reported last week, and the government is braced for further leaks, the science minister has said.Addressing a House of Lords debate on the attempted sale of data belonging to 500,000 UK Bio

stories.headline: Chile police blast water cannons at student protesters
stories.summary: Chile’s police has cracked down on student protests against the govt's proposals to limit free higher education.

stories.headline: UAE quits OPEC
stories.summary: The UAE announced it’s withdrawing from OPEC on May 1st.

stories.headline: New Florida map boosts Republican seats amid national redistricting fight
stories.summary: Florida has become latest state to redraw map, as national electoral landscape transforms before midterm elections.

stories.headline: Ukraine accuses Israel of receiving shipments of grain 'stolen' by Russia
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says it has received no evidence to support the claim that a vessel carrying grain from occupied Ukraine has reached Haifa.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.headline: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.summary: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.headline: NZ axes plan for WW2 sex slaves statue after Japan protest
stories.summary: Tokyo had warned that installing the structure in Auckland could jeopardise diplomatic relations.

stories.headline: James Comey indicted over 2025 social media post allegedly threatening Trump
stories.summary: The former FBI director shared a photo showing seashells arranged to write out “86 47,” which some saw as a call for violence against President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
stories.summary: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle EastThree vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry.The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker c

stories.headline: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
stories.summary: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
stories.summary: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.

stories.headline: US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal pleads not guilty to fraud charges
stories.summary: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.summary: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.summary: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: Shooting sparks safety fears ahead of World Cup
stories.summary: The Mexican government says the country is safe to visit for the World Cup.
